suppress.notes: matomo-java-tracker-core-3.0.6.jar: CVE-2023-6923 affects the Matomo PHP server, not the Java tracker client. False positive due to CPE/package name overlap.

False positive: CVE-2026-39882 and CVE-2026-39883 describe vulnerabilities in the OpenTelemetry Go SDK (memory exhaustion via unbounded OTLP HTTP response body, and BSD kenv PATH hijacking), not in the Java semconv conventions library. NVD's CPE matching hits io.opentelemetry.semconv because the product name "opentelemetry" matches both ecosystems. No Java fix exists or is needed.
False positive: see CVE-2026-39882 note above. Same Go-only advisory, same incorrect CPE match against the Java semconv artifact.

swagger-ui 5.32.2 bundles DOMPurify 3.3.2 which contains CVE-2026-41238 (prototype pollution XSS bypass via CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING). Fixed in DOMPurify 3.4.0 / swagger-ui 5.32.5. No springdoc release includes the fix yet (latest is 3.0.3 as of 2026-05-23). Revisit when springdoc > 3.0.3 is released.

swagger-ui 5.32.2 bundles DOMPurify 3.3.2 which contains CVE-2026-41239 (SAFE_FOR_TEMPLATES bypass via RETURN_DOM). Fixed in DOMPurify 3.4.0 / swagger-ui 5.32.5. No springdoc release includes the fix yet. See CVE-2026-41238 note above.

swagger-ui 5.32.2 bundles DOMPurify 3.3.2 which contains CVE-2026-41240 (FORBID_TAGS bypass when using function-based ADD_TAGS / tagCheck). Fixed in DOMPurify 3.4.0 / swagger-ui 5.32.5. No springdoc release includes the fix yet. See CVE-2026-41238 note above.

Same FORBID_TAGS/tagCheck logic flaw as CVE-2026-41240, reported by RetireJS without a CVE assigned yet. Affects DOMPurify 3.3.2 bundled in swagger-ui 5.32.2 (springdoc 3.0.3). Fixed in DOMPurify 3.4.0 / swagger-ui 5.32.5. No springdoc release includes the fix yet. Revisit when springdoc > 3.0.3 is released.
suppress.vulnerabilityName: .*FORBID_TAGS is not checked when a function-based ADD_TAGS.*
